_meta.description: Curated reference tokens for /why-token advisory skill. Extended with tokenomics blocks for /tokenomics Mode B. Maintained by Chase Wang.
_meta.notes: tokenomics.type: 'fair-launch' = no structured allocation. 'structured' = full vesting data. 'low-confidence' = partial/estimated data only, verify before use.

tokens.name: Dogecoin
tokens.summary: Started as a joke, built lasting community through meme culture and accessibility. Proved that community IS the product for attention-driven tokens.
tokens.lesson: Community engagement can sustain a token long-term even without formal value accrual — but it's the exception, not the rule.

tokenomics.note: Originally capped at 100B DOGE, cap removed Feb 2014. Now ~5.256B DOGE/year inflationary mining. No pre-mine, no ICO, no structured allocation or vesting.

tokens.name: Pepe
tokens.summary: Pure memecoin leveraging existing internet culture. Rapid community formation, no pretense of utility — honest about what it is.
tokens.lesson: Authenticity matters. Being honest about having no utility is more respected than faking it.

tokenomics.note: 420.69T fixed supply. 93.1% sent to Uniswap LP at launch (LP tokens burned). 6.9% retained in multi-sig for CEX listings and bridges — no formal vesting schedule. Fair launch by convention.

tokens.name: Fartcoin
tokens.summary: Demonstrates that absurdist branding can work when liquidity and timing align. Low-effort thesis, high-effort execution on launch mechanics.
tokens.lesson: Launch mechanics execution matters more than the thesis for attention-driven tokens.

tokenomics.note: 1B fixed supply. Launched via pump.fun bonding curve. Liquidity migrated to Raydium and LP burned at ~$69K market cap. No team allocation, no investor allocation, no vesting schedule.

tokens.name: Ethereum
tokens.summary: EIP-1559 introduced fee burning proportional to network usage, directly tying token scarcity to protocol activity. The canonical example of value accrual through burn mechanics.
tokens.lesson: Value accrual can be added after launch. ETH launched without a burn mechanism — EIP-1559 came years later when the network had real usage to capture.

tokenomics.source: Ethereum Foundation 2014 presale blog / genesis block
tokenomics.note: Genesis supply ~72M ETH. No cliff or vesting on any allocation — all unlocked at mainnet launch. ETH is now inflationary (~120M total) but original 2015 genesis was ~72M.

tokens.name: BNB
tokens.summary: Quarterly burns tied to Binance's exchange revenue plus utility as gas token on BNB Chain. Dual value accrual: revenue-linked burns + network utility.
tokens.lesson: Tying burns to real revenue creates a transparent, verifiable value accrual mechanism. Exchange tokens work when the exchange has real volume.

tokenomics.note: 200M original supply. Binance burns quarterly toward 100M target. Team tokens locked 1yr post-ICO then linear over next year. ICO investors fully liquid at TGE.

tokens.name: Pump.fun
tokens.summary: Platform fees flow directly to token holders. Simple, direct revenue capture — token value is tied to platform usage.
tokens.lesson: The simplest value accrual mechanisms are often the most effective. If users pay fees and the token captures them, the story is easy to tell.

tokenomics.note: Limited official disclosure. Community airdrop ~20% confirmed. Team/investor allocation and vesting not fully disclosed. Treat categories below as estimates only.

tokens.name: Kite AI
tokens.summary: Clean launch execution with strong initial liquidity and community coordination. Example of getting the mechanics right independent of token thesis.
tokens.lesson: Even with a strong product, a sloppy launch can kill momentum. Execution on launch day is its own discipline.

tokenomics.note: Minimal official tokenomics documentation available. All categories below are estimates and should be verified against official docs before use.

tokens.name: Solana
tokens.summary: Long development runway with fundamentals-first approach. Token launched when the network was ready, not when hype demanded it.
tokens.lesson: Patience pays. Launching when the product is ready — not when the market is hot — builds a stronger foundation.

tokenomics.note: Genesis supply ~488.6M SOL (now inflationary via staking rewards; ~590M as of 2024). Starting inflation rate 8%, decreasing 15%/yr toward 1.5% long-run. Seed and Series A rounds completed 2018-2019.

tokens.name: Bittensor
tokens.summary: Decentralized AI network with mining-based token distribution. Unique launch mechanism tied to the product's core function.
tokens.lesson: When your token distribution mechanism IS the product (mining = providing AI compute), the token and the product are inseparable.

tokenomics.note: 21M TAO hard cap (Bitcoin-inspired). No presale, no ICO, no VC allocation, no pre-mine. 100% distributed via proof-of-work style mining to validators and miners. Halving every ~10.5M blocks (~4 years). Opentensor Foundation has no special allocation.

tokens.name: Uniswap
tokens.summary: Retroactive airdrop to protocol users. Strategic timing, no public sale — turned existing users into token holders overnight.
tokens.lesson: You don't always need a sale. Rewarding existing users creates instant alignment and massive goodwill.

tokenomics.note: 1B UNI fixed supply. 4-year vesting for team, investors, advisors with 12-month cliff. Community treasury released gradually via governance over 4 years. Retroactive airdrop of 400 UNI to ~250K historical users was immediately claimable at TGE.

tokens.name: Chainlink
tokens.summary: Oracle network where LINK is staked as collateral by node operators and earns fees from data consumers. Token utility is directly tied to network usage — the more smart contracts need real-world data, the more LINK is required.
tokens.lesson: Infrastructure tokens work when the product has real enterprise demand. LINK took 5+ years to build the usage base that makes staking economics meaningful.

tokenomics.note: 1B LINK. 35% sold in ICO (fully liquid at TGE). 35% for node operator incentives released gradually by Chainlink Labs over 4+ years. 30% retained by team/company with 12-month cliff then linear vest. No formal cliff/vest was publicly committed at ICO — estimate based on actual release patterns.

tokens.name: Hyperliquid
tokens.summary: Perp DEX with a native L1. No VC, no public sale — 100% of token distributed to community and team. Protocol revenue funds the Assistance Fund which buys back and burns HYPE. The purest example of product-revenue-to-token-value linkage among 2024 launches.
tokens.lesson: No VC is a feature, not a bug, if you have real revenue and real users. Hyperliquid proved you can launch a top-10 token without a single institutional round.

tokenomics.source: Hyperliquid official docs and community posts around TGE (Nov 2024)
tokenomics.note: 1B HYPE. No VC, no private sale. ~31% genesis airdrop fully liquid at TGE. ~38.88% for future emissions/HLP/Assistance Fund. ~23.8% team with 1yr cliff then vest. Remaining ~6.32% future community distribution. Exact team vest schedule not fully disclosed — estimate based on available information.

tokens.name: Sky Protocol
tokens.summary: MakerDAO rebranded to Sky Protocol in 2024. $MKR converted to $SKY at 1:24,000 ratio. Protocol generates revenue from stability fees and DSR (DAI Savings Rate spread), which flows to token holders via burns and buybacks. One of the oldest and most battle-tested DeFi revenue models.
tokens.lesson: Brand pivots can unlock new communities, but the underlying token economics must evolve simultaneously. Sky's success depends on whether the new incentive structure (SubDAOs, SKY farming) retains the MakerDAO revenue quality.

tokenomics.note: 10B SKY total supply. MKR holders converted at 1:24,000 ratio — converted portion is immediately liquid. Ecosystem/SubDAO farming rewards vest over time via governance. Core contributor allocation has 12-month cliff. Confidence medium: rebrand was recent and full allocation schedules were still publishing.

tokens.name: Aave
tokens.summary: Lending protocol where AAVE is staked in the Safety Module as first-loss capital, earning protocol revenue. Token holders backstop bad debt in exchange for staking rewards — aligning incentives between protocol solvency and token holding.
tokens.lesson: Safety Module staking is one of the cleanest value accrual designs: token holders get paid to take on protocol risk. It only works if the protocol generates enough revenue to make the risk worth taking.

tokenomics.source: Aave Aavenomics proposal and token migration documentation (October 2020)
tokenomics.note: 16M AAVE total supply. Migrated from LEND (ETHLend) at 100 LEND = 1 AAVE. 13M (81.25%) available to LEND holders via migration — immediately liquid. 3M (18.75%) to Ecosystem Reserve for governance, Safety Module incentives, and grants — released gradually via governance over ~5 years.

tokens.name: Ondo Finance
tokens.summary: RWA (Real World Asset) protocol tokenizing US Treasuries and money market funds on-chain. Protocol earns management fees on AUM. Notable for its extremely low float at TGE (~2% circulating) — investor and team cliff created a 12-month window of artificially suppressed supply.
tokens.lesson: Low float at TGE is a double-edged sword: price discovery is distorted, but it rewards early community participants handsomely. The cliff date (12 months post-TGE) is when real price discovery begins.

tokenomics.note: 10B ONDO. Only ~2% circulating at TGE (protocol rewards + small community allocation). Investor and team unlock cliff is 12 months from TGE (January 2025), then linear vest. Low float intentional — rewards early community participants.

tokens.name: Ethena
tokens.summary: Synthetic dollar protocol (USDe) backed by delta-neutral crypto positions. Protocol captures funding rates from perpetual positions as yield, shared with sUSDe stakers. ENA captures governance rights and a share of protocol revenue. One of the fastest protocols to reach $1B+ AUM.
tokens.lesson: Yield-bearing stablecoins create a moat that pure stablecoins can't match — but the yield depends on perpetual funding rates being positive. The token thesis breaks if funding rates go negative for extended periods.

tokenomics.source: Ethena Labs tokenomics documentation and TGE announcement (April 2024)
tokenomics.note: 15B ENA. ~5% (750M) unlocked at TGE via Season 1 airdrop. Investors and team have 12-month cliff from TGE. Ecosystem development allocation releases over 4 years. Binance Labs and major VCs in investor tranche.

tokens.name: Morpho
tokens.summary: Lending optimization layer on top of Compound and Aave, later evolving into a standalone lending primitive. Token launched non-transferable in October 2024 — transferability enabled later by governance. Protocol earns fees from lending markets it powers.
tokens.lesson: Launching a non-transferable token kills day-1 speculation but builds genuine stakeholder alignment. It's a credible commitment mechanism — only works if the protocol is strong enough that people want to hold despite illiquidity.

tokenomics.source: Morpho Labs tokenomics documentation (October 2024 token launch)
tokenomics.note: 1B MORPHO. Launched non-transferable — transferability enabled by governance vote post-launch. Community/users receive tokens via rewards but can't trade initially. Investors and team have 12-month cliff. Confidence medium due to non-transferable launch making TGE semantics nuanced.

tokens.name: LayerZero
tokens.summary: Omnichain interoperability protocol. ZRO is used to pay messaging fees for cross-chain transactions. Novel anti-sybil airdrop mechanism: users had to donate a small amount of USDC to claim ('proof of donation'). Fee capture grows as cross-chain volume grows.
tokens.lesson: The proof-of-donation anti-sybil mechanism was controversial but effective. It raised ~$15M for Protocol Guild while filtering out low-conviction farmers. A first-mover design that may become a template.

tokenomics.source: LayerZero tokenomics documentation and TGE details (June 2024)
tokenomics.note: 1B ZRO. ~8.5% distributed via airdrop at TGE (proof-of-donation anti-sybil mechanism). Investors and team have 12-month cliff. LayerZero Foundation reserves large ecosystem fund for future grants and incentives.

tokens.name: Jupiter
tokens.summary: Largest DEX aggregator on Solana. JLP (Jupiter Liquidity Pool) earns trading fees. Protocol revenue shared with JLP holders and used for ecosystem development. Notable for its 50% team allocation with 2-year hard lockup — one of the most founder-aligned vesting structures in 2024.
tokens.lesson: A 2-year hard lockup with no early releases is a credibility signal. Jupiter's team put their money where their mouth is — and the market rewarded it. Contrast with projects that vest team tokens monthly from day 1.

tokenomics.source: Jupiter (jup.ag) tokenomics and Jupuary airdrop documentation (January 2024)
tokenomics.note: 10B JUP. 40% immediately circulating at TGE: 10% Jupuary airdrop + 10% future airdrops (over time) + 20% initial liquidity. 50% team/core contributors: 2-year hard lockup (no release until Jan 2026), then 2-year linear vest. 10% community/strategic reserve with 6-month cliff.
